prompt: A RailCommerce Operations iOS system designed for fully offline ticket and merchandise sales, controlled content publishing, membership marketing, customer service workflows, secure local messaging, and offline talent matching for staffing. The system supports multiple roles including Customer/Member (browses content, purchases tickets and products, manages after-sales), Sales Agent (handles transactions and inventory), Content Editor and Reviewer (two-step approval workflow for publishing), Customer Service Representative (manages after-sales requests and messaging), and Administrator (controls system configuration and access). Customers can browse locally published travel advisories and onboard offers organized by configurable taxonomy (region, theme, rider type), manage a full CRUD cart, and receive bundle suggestions. A deterministic promotion pipeline enforces at most three discounts, prevents stacking multiple percent-off coupons, and records the final applied calculation with line-level explanations. Checkout supports shipping templates, saved US addresses, invoice notes, and tamper protection via hashing immutable order snapshots stored in the Keychain, with idempotent submission using a client-generated order ID and a 10-second duplicate lockout. After-sales flows include return-and-refund, refund-only, and exchange processes with reason selection, photo proof capture via camera permissions, SLA timers (first response within 4 business hours, resolution within 3 business days), and automation rules such as auto-approving refunds under $25 after 48 hours without dispute and auto-rejecting requests after 14 days past service date, with closed-loop messaging and local notifications only. Staff communication uses offline peer-to-peer messaging with queued delivery, contact masking, sensitive data blocking (SSN and payment card patterns), attachment limits (10MB, JPEG/PNG/PDF), and anti-harassment controls. The system includes a seat-inventory engine modeling availability by train, date, segment, and class, with atomic transactions, 15-minute reservation locks, and daily snapshots for auditing and rollback. Content publishing supports versioning (up to 10 versions), draft-review-publish-rollback lifecycle, scheduled publishing via Background Tasks, and battery-optimized heavy processing. Attachments are stored in the app sandbox with a 30-day cleanup policy. Talent matching operates fully offline with resume import, on-device indexing, Boolean filtering, weighted ranking (skills 50%, experience 30%, certifications 20%), saved searches, bulk tagging, and explainable match results. The app enforces cold start under 1.5 seconds on iPhone 11-class devices, handles memory warnings with cache eviction and deferred decoding, and ensures responsive UI.
backend_framework: Realm (local persistence layer) with RxSwift for reactive flows
database: Realm (encrypted on-device storage) and iOS Keychain (for secrets, encryption keys, and order hash storage)
